section_title: 10 DETAILS OF THE UNDERLYING SCENARIOS USED TO CALCULATE THE IMPACTS

scenarios.title: A1 – raw material supply
scenarios.description: Module A1 comprises impacts from extraction and processing of raw materials including bitumen, polymers, reinforcements (glass- and polyester fibre), fillers, etc. The recycling process of secondary raw materials used in the product is also included in module A1.

scenarios.title: A2 – transport to the manufacturer
scenarios.description: The raw materials are transported to the manufacturing site. Primary data are provided by Derbigum.

scenarios.title: A3 – manufacturing
scenarios.description: Module A3 includes the manufacturing of the final product, production and transport of larger packaging materials (pallets), combustion of fuels on site (natural gas, diesel, and propane), as well as end-of-life treatment of waste generated during manufacturing. It also includes the production of electricity (Derbigum mix).

scenarios.title: A4 – transport to the building site
scenarios.description:
Fuel type and consumption of vehicle or vehicle type used for transport: Truck 16-32 ton, 0,256 l diesel / km
Distance: 100 km and 100 km+ 35 km
Capacity utilisation (including empty returns): Default ecoinvent
Bulk density of transported products: Default ecoinvent
Volume capacity utilisation factor: Default ecoinvent

Default B-PCR distances for delivery are used, completed with specific data provided by Derbigum for the repartition of the transport. The following transport steps apply:
– 20% directly to the construction site over 100 km with a 16-32 ton lorry (ecoinvent record: 'Transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U')
– 80% to a supplier over 100 km with a 16 -32 ton lorry (ecoinvent record: 'Transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U')
– 100% of these 80% is transported over 35 km from supplier to construction site with a 16-32 ton lorry (ecoinvent record: 'Transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U')

scenarios.title: A5 – installation in the building
scenarios.description:
The product can be used on different types of substrates: wood, concrete, and sheet steel. It can also be installed on a vapour- barrier layer such as Derbicoat NT 2.5 (see B-EPD 24.0156.01- 01.00.00) to form a double-layer roof waterproofing coating solution.

The installation includes an overlap of the layers (10.5%) for complete waterproofing. It is integrated in the in the FU (in modules A1, A2, A3, and A4) of the present B-EPD.

The installation mode is "fully torched by use of flame" (torched with flame). The production and combustion of propane for torching are counted (+ associated CO2 emissions). The amount of propane for 1 m² is 0,150 kg/m² (= kg/FU) (prEN 17388 by use of flame:2022).

Waste from the installation (trimming waste, 2%) is classified as material for recycling (95%)- and waste for landfill (5%). This waste is measured for 1 m² "net installed" (0,02 m²/FU).

At the construction site, packaging materials are released. The impacts from waste management processes are included in module A5, whereas potential benefits from material reuse/recycling and energy recovery from incineration are reported in module D.

Parts of the installation:
- Processes necessary for the installation of the product: Manual installation
- Fixation materials: 0,150 kg/FU Propane for torching
- Jointing materials: -
- Treatments: -
- Material losses: 9,006 E-02 kg/FU Trimming waste (2%) (0,02 m²/FU)
- Packaging: 0,1406 kg/FU Pallet waste (including installation waste and overlap)
- Others: 10,5% Additional sheet for overlap on the layers for complete waterproofing (already included the FU)
- Ancillary materials for installation (specified by material): Insert information
- Water use: -
- Other resource use: Propane 0,150 kg/FU
- Quantitative description of energy type (regional mix) and consumption during the installation process: -
- Waste materials on the building site before waste processing, generated by the product's installation (specified by type): Trimming waste 9,006 E-02 kg/FU 2% (0,02 m²/FU); Packaging waste: pallet 0,1406 kg/FU
- Output materials (specified by type) as result of waste processing at the building site e.g. of collection for recycling, for energy recovery, disposal (specified by route): Trimming waste 9,006 E-02 kg/FU 95% recycled by Derbigum, 5% "non sorted waste: landfill"
- Direct emissions to ambient air, soil and water: None
- Distance: Derbigum recycling facility 128 km* 8,555 E-2 kg/FU; Sorting centre 30 km 4,503 E-03 kg/FU** 1,406 kg/FU***; Landfill 50 km 4,503 E-03 kg/FU**; Incineration 100 km 5,625 E-02 kg/FU***
* Corresponding to the average A4 transport distance
** 5% installation waste
*** Pallet waste

scenarios.title: B1 – Use
scenarios.description: No information could be collected on the emissions of the products to air or water during their use.

scenarios.title: B2 – Maintenance
scenarios.description: No product maintenance takes place during the reference service life.

scenarios.title: B3 - Repair
scenarios.description: No product repair takes place during the reference service life. Planned replacement is accounted in module B4.

scenarios.title: B4 – Replacement
scenarios.description:
The Derbigum NT 4 has to be replaced after 40 years to ensure a correct waterproofing. In practice, the existing layer is left in place and an additional layer of Derbigum NT 4 is added.

The inventory included the amount of Derbigum NT 4 for 1 m² including overlap (10,5%), the installation losses (2%), and the propane for fully torched installation on the existing layer.

The waste includes the installation losses and the packaging waste.

The scenario is identical to the installation A5:
Materials:
− Derbigum NT 4: production and transport of 1 m² + overlapping 10,5%, + 2% losses [A1,A2,A3,A4]
Installation:
− Ancillary material: 0,150 kg propane/m² + associated CO2 emissions
− Derbigum NT 4 waste: 95% of the installation losses are recycled as Gumix APP and 5% are landfilled
− Pallet waste: the BE-PCR scenario for pallet end of life is respected
− Transport of the waste: as in A5

The impacts from waste management processes are included in module B4, whereas potential benefits from material reuse/recycling and energy recovery from incineration are reported in module D.

scenarios.title: B5 - Refurbishment
scenarios.description: No product refurbishment takes place during the reference service life.

scenarios.title: B6 – Operational energy use
scenarios.description: The product does not use energy during its use.

scenarios.title: B7 – Operational water use
scenarios.description: The product does not use water during its use.

scenarios.title: C1: Deconstruction
scenarios.description: The waterproofing layer is removed manually, and no impacts are readily attributed to the product whereby no emissions arise from the deconstruction

scenarios.title: C2: Transport
scenarios.description:
Comprises impacts from transportation of the deconstructed products after 60 years to the waste processing (sorting centre and landfill, or recycling site).

The amount of material to consider includes the overlap, and the second layer added as replacement in module B4 (and its overlap). It corresponds to 2 x 1 FU.

Module C2 – Transport to waste processing:
- Type of vehicle: Lorry 16-32 t EURO5
- Fuel consumption: EI (ecoinvent default value)
- Distance to Derbigum facility: 128 km
- Distance to Sorting centre: 30 km
- Distance to Landfill: 50 km
- Capacity utilisation: EI
- Density of products: 1126 kg / m3

scenarios.title: C3: Waste processing
scenarios.description:
Derbigum roofs sold in Belgian (or foreign) market come with a "take back" guarantee, which means all losses during installation are collected and transported back to Derbigum recycling facility in Belgium. It is assumed that 95% of the Derbigum NT 4 waste are recovered and recycled.

The burden for recycling process and the benefits from replacing bitumen are recorded in Module D.

The remaining 5% are considered to be mixed with the other "non sorted" waste of the deconstruction site. They go to the sorting centre where they are handled or moved once. Default values for BE-PCR are applied.

End-of-life modules – C3 and C4:
- Wastes collected separately: 9,454 kg/FU
- Wastes collected as mixed construction waste: 0,4976 kg/FU
- Waste for re-use: -
- Waste for recycling: 9,454 kg/FU
- Waste for energy recovery: -
- Waste for final disposal: 0,4976 kg/FU

scenarios.title: C4: Disposal
scenarios.description: The residual 5% of membrane waste are landfilled in this module.

scenarios.title: D – Benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries
scenarios.description:
Module D is calculated in accordance with EN 15804:2012+A2:2019 and BE-PCR. All reported net benefits and loads resulting from net flows leaving the system of products that have not been assigned as co-products (there are none in this case) and have reached end-of-waste status are included in the Module D (reuse, recycling, direct energy recovery).

Production, trimming and end of life waste of Derbigum NT 4:
At end of life most of the product is recycled (patented process by Derbigum, and organised collection network).
– A5, B4, C3: Installation and demolition waste: 95% is recycled in Derbigum facility.
– The load for recycling is included (recycling unit is modelled with primary data)
– Avoided material: The net flow of recycled material is calculated according to BE-PCR, and the corresponding amount of virgin bitumen is evaluated taking the efficiency of the recycling process into account.

Pallets: A5, B4: 0,2813 kg/FU; PCI = 12,24 MJ/kg
– Reuse: 20%: 5,625 E-02 kg/FU; transport 20 km
– Recycling (wood chips): 40% 0,1125 kg/FU; transport 20 km + chipping.
– Incineration: 40%; 0,1125 kg/FU.

Energy recovery: efficiency
– Pallet waste: incineration in a specific cogeneration unit, energy efficiency of 50% for thermal energy and 22% for electric energy.

QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE LOADS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES:
– Recycling process for bituminous membrane waste
– Transport for reused pallets: 20 km
– Transport for recycled pallets: 20 km
– Wood chipping of pallets

QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE BENEFITS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES:
– Avoided virgin bitumen: 4,112 kg/FU
– Pallet reused: 5,625 E-02 kg/FU
– Wood chips (pallet recycled): 0,1125 kg/FU
– Energy recovery:
o Heat: 0,6885 MJ/FU (substitution: thermal energy from natural gas)
o Electricity: 1,091 kWh/FU (or 0,3029 MJ/FU)(substitution: BE high voltage mix, excluding impact from the transmission network)
